Alright, JB, time for your daily motivation slash skills training. I want to tell you a funny story here. If you're in certification, but if you're not in certification, please sign up. You're just out of your mind, alright? It's such an awesome program and the impact it's having on people's lives is ridiculous, right? Here's the deal. If you're wondering how I got the idea to use that footage of the prospector, right? It was from Buster Skaggs, this hysterically ridiculous movie I watched on Netflix. And I saw this scene, this old tin pan miner, and he's, you know, it is panning in a fast running stream sifting for gold, sifting through water for gold, sifting for gold, right? And he finds a little nugget of great. What does that tell you about how I go through life? I want to kind of talk about the inner and the outer game here. The fact that I saw that months and months ago and then used that in the footage, I spliced it in, enough of it in there so you can get the idea, I am constantly on the lookout here as I move through the world for things that will help me succeed in life. Things I can use, things I can pull from. Are you doing it? Like are you going through the world right now with just, you know, laser guided focus on what you want in life and just, you're never really looking at the bigger picture. Start looking at the bigger picture guys. Like some of the things that I do that allow me to succeed, it has to do with simply just at every moment I'm awake, I'm looking, I'm analyzing, I'm saying what can I make of what's around me to further advance my outcome for my life? I don't go through life passively. I go through life at a very high level. I go through life in a very directed way in a very deliberate way. Now I understand we're going through prospecting, right? But the bottom line is, is what are you doing as you drive around in your car, walking in the streets, those, when I'm selling something everywhere I go, I don't care what I'm selling in person or on the phone. I'm looking at street, I'm looking at signs. I'm looking at the way neighborhoods are configured. I'm saying, how can I find buyers for what I'm selling? Are you doing that? Are you going through life right now? Going out to eat, going shopping in a mall, you know, just going into Starbucks? Are you doing it from the perspective of like just an average person moving for life or you're a directed businessman always on the go looking, analyzing, scanning for opportunity? See, when you're a prospector, when you're really a prospector, you can't help but always trying to find gold wherever you go. Wherever you go, you're looking for opportunity and you never know where you're going to find it. You might find customers wherever you, who knows? You're all in different industries, right? But the point is, is that I want you to start looking at the world through the eyes of almost a predator. Not an evil one. And I don't want you to say like, you know, you're this evil carnivore, you're looking to hurt. I don't mean that. But, you know, as a salesperson, we eat what we kill. Seriously, it's a good, it's a powerful metaphor, right? And again, nothing about ethics and integrity. You have to maintain that. But the point is, is that you eat what you kill and are you scanning? Are you looking? Are you zeroing in all the stuff around you that you could actually use to close sales? There's customers everywhere. I understand maybe you're just calling on the phone in a very directed fashion. You'd be getting leads on life fair enough. You've probably already been through the sales funnel training right now. It's awesome training how you do sales for us. That's amazing, right? But how are you moving through the world? I don't believe that you could truly be successful if you are saying, I work from nine to five or six or seven. And when I'm off, I like put blinders on and stop trying to seize opportunity. Opportunity presents itself every moment of the day. You never know when it's going to happen. At all times of the night, at all times of the year, you have to be willing to go out and get it and you're constantly looking for it. I'm a prospector for opportunity as much as anything else in my life. Think about that. And make sure right now when you're going through these lessons one by one that you're like just like put your wallet into it. Don't be a 10 watt light bulb burning at half mass. You're barely just, oh wow, it's great. Yay. Spoon feeding me how to do this. Make the most of this. I know that I'm going to bet unless you're that really, really rare breed. You didn't pay attention in schools as much as you should have. Right? I didn't. I wish I could go back and have really paid attention to everything that was being taught to me, even though it was fricking boring back then, right? But the point is now I make it interesting. I keep you on the edge of the seat so you'll listen. But the point is this is your chance. It's the gift you're giving yourself. Raise up your knowledge rate. Elevate yourself.
